description_long: This object is a compact, antique-style shotgun with a dark wooden handle and a short, thick barrel. The barrel appears to be made of metal, possibly steel, and is finished with a matte, dark coating. The handle is ergonomically curved and features a brass or gold-colored spherical end cap and matching trigger guard accents. The trigger mechanism is exposed, and the hammer is prominently visible above the grip, suggesting a flintlock or percussion cap design. The overall form is robust and utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements, emphasizing function over ornamentation. The shotgun's size and design indicate it is a single-barrel, hand-held firearm, likely intended for close-range use.
description: A compact, antique-style shotgun with a dark wooden handle, short metal barrel, and brass accents. The exposed hammer and trigger mechanism highlight its classic, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the curved wooden handle, brass end cap, exposed hammer, and short, dark metal barrel.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the circular barrel opening and the alignment of the handle and barrel.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the handle and barrel details, with the trigger and hammer still visible.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the rounded brass end cap and the back of the handle.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, short length, no visible ribbing or venting
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized, simple with no finger grooves or reinforcement
